name: Medicaid Enrolled Provider Listing
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: PROVIDER EMAIL
columns.description: The provider email address related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID. Email address will only be included for Provider Duty Nursing (PDN) providers. All other provider records will be blank.

columns.name: FILE DATE
columns.description: Date the file was created at eMedNY.  mm/dd/ccyy with leading zeros

columns.name: MEDICALLY FRAGILE CHILDREN AND ADULTS DIRECTORY IND
columns.description: Indicator to identify provider enrolled in the Medically Fragile Children Directory. Indicator will be Y for Yes and N for No

columns.name: MEDICAID PROVIDER ID
columns.description: A unique number assigned to each provider enrolled to provide services to members of the Medicaid program. This number is the primary method of identifying a provider.

columns.name: STATE
columns.description: State related to the service address for the MMIS ID.

columns.name: NPI
columns.description: National Provider ID (NPI) is a standard unique identifier for health care providers. This NPI relates to the MMIS ID and is the most current NPI on file.

columns.name: PROVIDER OR FACILITY NAME
columns.description: The name of a provider of Medicaid services as used on official State records.

columns.name: MEDICAID TYPE
columns.description: FFS (Fee for Service Medicaid): Provider is enrolled and participating in the Medicaid fee for service program. The provider may or may not be a participating provider in the Medicaid managed care program.  MCO (Managed Care Only): Provider is enrolled but cannot bill the Medicaid fee for service program. The provider is a participating provider in the Medicaid managed care program.  OPRA (Ordering, Prescribing, Referring, Attending):  Provider is enrolled and participating in the Medicaid program as a non-billing provider.

columns.name: PROFESSION OR SERVICE
columns.description: Specifies the broad areas of service that a provider can render.

columns.name: PROVIDER SPECIALTY
columns.description: Identifies the medical specialty for which a provider is certified.

columns.name: SERVICE ADDRESS
columns.description: Address related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID.

columns.name: CITY
columns.description: The City related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID.

columns.name: ZIP CODE
columns.description: Zip Code related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID.

columns.name: COUNTY
columns.description: County related to the service address for the MMIS ID.

columns.name: TELEPHONE
columns.description: Telephone number related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: Latitude related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID. –nn.dddddddddddd (example: Zip Code 10029-4623 would reflect Latitude 40.7920468)

columns.name: LONGITUDE
columns.description: Longitude related to the service address for the Medicaid provider ID. nnn.dddddddddddd (example: Zip Code 10029-4623 would reflect Longitude ¬-73.9444103)

columns.name: ENROLLMENT BEGIN DATE
columns.description: Most recent date the provider began participating in NYS FFS Medicaid/OPRA/ Managed Care Only. mm/dd/ccyy with leading zeros

columns.name: NEXT ANTICIPATED REVALIDATION DATE
columns.description: Estimated date by which the provider is required to revalidate.  May be subject to change based on several factors.

description: Revalidation disclaimer: The next anticipated revalidation date is an estimated date that may be subject to change based on factors such as a timing lag, updates made to an enrollment file prior to revalidation, and the State’s ability to extend the date for certain providers. Therefore, providers should make sure the correspondence address on their enrollment file is current, and providers should not submit a revalidation package until they receive a notice in the mail to do so. name: New York State Statewide COVID-19 Testing (Archived)
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Cumulative Number of Tests Performed
columns.description: Running total for the number of tests of individuals performed in each county as of the last update to the dataset. This total includes positives, negatives, and inconclusive results.

columns.name: Test % Positive
columns.description: For the dates before 4/4/2022: The percentage of all COVID tests reported to NYSDOH that are positive. For the dates 4/4/2022 and forward: The percentage of PCR COVID tests performed that are positive.

columns.name: Total Number of Tests Performed
columns.description: The number of tests of individuals performed on the test date in each county. This total includes positives, negatives, and inconclusive results.

columns.name: Cumulative Number of Positives
columns.description: Running total for the number of persons tested positive for COVID-19 infection in each county as of the test date.

columns.name: New Positives
columns.description: The number of new persons tested positive for COVID-19 infection on the test date in each county.

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county of residence for the person tested.

columns.name: Test Date
columns.description: The date the test result was processed by the NYS Electronic Clinical Laboratory Reporting System (ECLRS).

columns.name: Geography
columns.description: Identifies the level of geographical aggregation of a record. Values are: COUNTY, REGION, or STATEWIDE

description: Note: This dataset is no longer being updated as of September 1, 2023. This dataset includes information on the number of tests of individuals for COVID-19 infection performed in New York State beginning March 1, 2020, when the first case of COVID-19 was identified in the state. The primary goal of publishing this dataset is to provide users timely information about local disease spread and reporting of positive cases. description: This data includes the name and location of food service establishments and the violations that were found at the time of their last inspection. Although violation details are collected on inspection reports (i.e., the actual food item, quantity and temperature of food found out of temperature control) as well as corrective actions for critical violations, this data set is limited to the violation number and the corresponding general violation description. This dataset is for reporting purposes

description: The Genealogical Research Death Index assists individuals with locating New York State (NYS) death records that fall within defined genealogy years, exclusive of New York City recorded death records. This Index contains information on decedents, date of death, gender, age at death, and NYS file number that will allow the public to search for individuals on a variety of genealogy criteria if on file for at least 50 years.

description: The New York State Department of Health Radon Program contracts with a radon testing laboratory to provide short-term charcoal radon test kits, radon test kit analysis, and results to residents. The contract laboratory provides the radon test results to the individual home owner and the Radon Program. All testing data is entered into our database. From this database, we are able to create radon prevalence maps, design special outreach activities and campaigns, and track the location in the home

columns.name: Procedure
columns.description:
Type of procedure performed:  All PCI, Non-Emergency PCI, Emergency PCI, CABG, or Valve Surgery.  PCI stands for Percutaneous Coronary Intervention, sometimes also called coronary angioplasty or coronary stenting.
CABG stands for Coronary Artery Bypass Graft surgery.

columns.name: Upper Limit of Confidence Interval
columns.description: Upper 95% confidence limit for risk adjusted mortality rate.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: Region of hospital performing the procedure.

columns.name: Lower Limit of Confidence Interval
columns.description: Lower 95% confidence limit for risk adjusted mortality rate.

columns.name: Risk-Adjusted Mortality Rate
columns.description: The best estimate, based on the statistical model, of what the provider’s mortality rate would have been if the provider had a mix of patients similar to the statewide mix. It is obtained by first dividing the observed mortality rate by the expected mortality rate, and then multiplying that quotient by the statewide mortality rate.

columns.name: Expected Mortality Rate
columns.description: The sum of the predicted probabilities of death for all patients divided by the total number of patients.

columns.name: Observed Mortality Rate
columns.description: The observed number of deaths divided by the total number of cases

columns.name: Number of Deaths
columns.description: Number of cases resulting in death during the hospitalization or after discharge but within 30-days.

columns.name: Number of Cases
columns.description: Number of procedures performed.

columns.name: Comparison Results
columns.description: A statistical test was performed to determine if each hospital performed significantly better or worse than average for the given year. Result may be: Significantly higher than statewide rate, Significantly lower than statewide rate, Not different than the statewide rate

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description:
New York State (NYS) Facility Identification Number.
Facility ID ‘0000’ represents the sum (or average) of all the reported data in NYS

description: This dataset contains the number of cases, number of in hospital/30 day deaths, observed, expected and risk- adjusted mortality rates for cardiac surgery and percutaneous coronary interventions (PCI) by hospital. Regions represent where the hospitals are located. For additional information, visit: http://www.health.ny.gov/health_care/consumer_information/cardiac_surgery/

columns.name: Percent
columns.description: Total number of children who resided in the zip code that were identified for the first time with confirmed elevated lead blood levels within the specified year, divided by the total number of children who resided in the zip code that had a blood lead screening test in that same year multiplied by 100.

columns.name: Rate per 1,000
columns.description: Total number of children who resided in the zip code that were identified for the first time with confirmed elevated lead blood levels within the specified year, divided by the total number of children who resided in the zip code that had a blood lead screening test in that same year multiplied by 1,000.

name: Hospital-Acquired Infections: Beginning 2008
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Indicator Name
columns.description: Central line-associated blood stream infection (CLABSI) indicators

columns.name: Year
columns.description:
Year of data. Hospital‐specific pilot year data are not included. Thus, start dates are 2008 for CLABSIs in ICUs, colon SSIs,
CABG SSIs, and hip SSIs; 2010 for C. difficile; 2013 for hysterectomy SSIs, 2014 for CRE, and 2015 for CLABSIs in wards. All
hospitals with complete data for a given year are included. In the case of hospital closures, data are provided up to the last
complete year prior to closure.

columns.name: Infections Observed
columns.description:
Number of infections reported by hospital in the given year.
Result is shown as long as at least 20 procedures were performed (for SSI data) or at least 50 central line days were
reported (for CLABSI data).

columns.name: Infections Predicted
columns.description:
The number of infections predicted to occur in the hospital based on average infection rates in NYS among similar patients
in the same year.
For CLABSIs, the number of predicted infections is calculated using CLABSI rates within each location (and NICU
birthweight category) in NYS in a given year. Result is shown if at least 50 central line days were reported.
For SSIs, the number of predicted infections is calculated from procedure‐specific logistic regression models (see annual
NYS reports for risk factors included in the models). Result is shown as long as at least 20 procedures were performed.
For CDI, the number of predicted infections is calculated from regression models (see annual NYS reports for risk factors
included in the models).
CRE data were not risk adjusted so the number of predicted infections is not shown.
No results are shown for “New York State – All Hospitals” since the predicted number is the same as the observed number.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description:
For CLABSI indicators, this is the number of central line days.
For SSI indicators, this is the number of procedures.
For CDI hospital onset prior to 2015, this is the number of patient days at risk (i.e. excluding first three days of admission)
excluding newborns and NICU.
For CDI hospital onset in 2015, this is the number of patient days excluding newborns and NICU.
Hospital‐Acquired Infection Data
DATA DICTIONARY
December 2016
NYS Department of Health, Bureau of Healthcare‐Associated Infections Pg. 4 of 9
For CDI Possibly‐My‐Hospital‐associated, this is the number of patient days excluding newborns and NICU.
For CDI Community Onset Not‐My‐Hospital, this is the number of admissions excluding newborns and NICU.
For CRE Hospital Onset, this is the number of patient days.
For CRE admission prevalence, this is the number of admissions.
For all CDI and CRE indicators, beginning in 2015 all denominators exclude inpatient rehabilitation units and inpatient
psychiatric units with different Centers for Medicaid and Medicare Services Certification Numbers CCNs.

columns.name: Indicator Value
columns.description:
For CLABSI indicators within ICUs, this is the unadjusted CLABSI rate.
For SSI procedure‐specific indicators, this is the risk adjusted SSI rate.
For CLABSI and SSI SIRs, this is the SIR.
For CDI hospital onset, this is the adjusted CDI rate.
For CDI Community Onset Not‐My‐Hospital and Possibly‐My‐Hospital associated, these are the unadjusted CDI rates.
For CRE, these are unadjusted CRE rates.

columns.name: Indicator Lower Confidence Limit
columns.description: Lower 95% confidence limit for indicator.

columns.name: Indicator Upper Confidence Limit
columns.description: Upper 95% confidence limit for indicator.

columns.name: Indicator Units
columns.description:
Describes numerator and denominator used to calculate indicator. The SIR does not have units because it is the number of
observed infections divided by the number of predicted infections.

columns.name: Comparison Results
columns.description:
For CLABSI, SSI, and hospital onset CDI a statistical test was performed to determine if each hospital performed
significantly better or worse than average for the given year. Result may be:
Significantly higher than NYS average,
Significantly lower than NYS average,
Not significantly different than NYS average,
Not compared ‐ if fewer than 20 procedures (SSI) or 50 central line days (CLABSI), or specialty hospital (CDI).

provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Disease
columns.description: When the laboratory test can discern virus type (type A or type B), cases are counted by the corresponding disease (“Influenza A” or “Influenza B”). When the virus type cannot be differentiated, cases are counted as “Influenza Unspecified”.

columns.name: County Centroid
columns.description: The mapping coordinates (latitude, longitude) for the center of the specified county.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The five regions in New York are defined by county as Capital District Region counties: Albany, Clinton, Columbia, Delaware, Essex, Franklin, Fulton, Greene, Hamilton, Montgomery, Otsego, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie, Warren, Washington Central Region counties: Broome, Cayuga, Chenango, Cortland, Herkimer, Jefferson, Lewis, Madison, Oneida, Onondaga, Oswego, St Lawrence, Tioga, Tompkins Metropolitan Region counties: Dutchess, Nassau, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Suffolk, Sullivan, Ulster, Westchester New York City counties/boroughs: Bronx, Kings, New York, Queens, Richmond Western Region counties: Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Chemung, Erie, Genesee, Livingston, Monroe, Niagara, Ontario, Orleans, Schuyler, Seneca, Steuben, Wayne, Wyoming, Yates

columns.name: Season
columns.description: Because influenza activity peaks in winter, the influenza season is named for the two calendar years over which a single influenza epidemic spans. CDC defines the influenza season as beginning with week 40 (generally the first week in October) of one calendar year and ending with week 20 of the following calendar year (generally the third week in May).

columns.name: County
columns.description: Cases are assigned to a county based on this order of preference: 1) the patient’s address, 2) the ordering healthcare provider’s address, or 3) the ordering facility’s address.

columns.name: CDC Week
columns.description: CDC designates each week of the year with a sequential number starting with 1 to a maximum of 52 or 53. Week 1 is the first week of the year that has at least four days in the calendar year. CDC defines the influenza season as beginning with week 40 (generally the first week in October) and ending with week 20 of the following calendar year (generally the third week in May).  Also known as MMWR week.

columns.name: Week Ending Date
columns.description: The last date of each CDC week. Each week begins on Sunday and ends on Saturday; week ending dates are always on a Saturday.

columns.name: Count
columns.description: The number of laboratory-confirmed influenza cases reported to NYSDOH for the corresponding season, week, disease, and county.

columns.name: FIPS
columns.description: Federal Information Processing Series (FIPS) geographic codes are assigned to each state and county; a unique county-level geographic code.

description: This dataset includes weekly counts of laboratory-confirmed influenza (flu) cases, by county and influenza type, going back to the 2009-10 season. The primary goal of this dataset is to provide users timely information about local influenza activity throughout the current influenza season. Although each influenza season is unique, historical data is also provided for trend comparison. Cases are included that meet the New York State Department of Health’s (NYSDOH) standardized case definition, i.

description: The dataset contains the location, legionella sampling results and operational status of cooling towers operating in New York State. The data is self-reported by the cooling tower operator according to Public Health Law, Section 225(5)(a) Subpart 4-1 – Cooling Towers. This dataset is a weekly snapshot of the data collected from the NYS Cooling Tower Registry Website it does not contain historical data.

denominator.

appropriate measurement year.)

description: The Managed Long-Term Care (MLTC) performance data is an annual evaluation (semi-annual prior to 2022) of New York state-certified MLTC plans. Rates are calculated for each performance measure by plan and describe their population, quality of care, and enrollees’ satisfaction. These data also present the statewide demographic profile of MLTC enrollees and statewide rates of emergency room, hospital, and nursing home utilization. columns.description: The results for each indicator are grouped into the “top 20%,” the “second highest 20%,” etc.; these are referred to as “quintiles.”  This field displays these findings for each clinic. ("First" is the highest; "Fifth" is the lowest.)

Classification of the provider organization. Values include "Community Health Center," "Designated
AIDS Center," "Drug Treatment Center" and "Hospital (non‐DAC)."

columns.description: The percentage of “yes” responses (“score”) for each indicator.

columns.description:
Number of patients in the sample for the clinic that were eligible for each particular indicator. If 5 or
fewer, this field and the Indicator_value field are redacted.

columns.name: Year
columns.description:
Calendar year of clinical care under review. For example, "2011" represents a review in 2012 of clinical
care provided between 1/1/2011 and 12/31/2011.

columns.name: Master ID
columns.description: A code used to identify indicators and allow comparisons across years when indicators have stayed essentially the same despite possible name changes.

columns.description:
The name of the clinic providing care. Separate reviews are conducted for each site within a multi‐site
organization. Program names are also generated for regional and statewide averages.

columns.name: Region
columns.description:
The geographic region in which each clinic is located. Each New York City borough constitutes a region;
there are five other regions for the rest of the state.

description: This dataset contains monthly snapshots of the number of enrollments by population in the New York State Donate Life Registry beginning from 2008. The registry is a confidential database that records a person’s consent to organ/tissue donation upon death. The database is used by donation professionals to determine a person's donation status at the time of their death.

description: This data includes the name and location of inactive food service establishments and the violations that were found at the time of the inspection. Inactive food service establishments include only establishments that are no longer in business or have not operated for an extended period of time. Inspections are a “snapshot” in time and are not always reflective of the day-to-day operations and overall condition of an establishment. This dataset is refreshed on a monthly basis.

columns.description:
Statistical significance of comparison: RAR to Statewide Rate. 
Values:  Above, Below, NS (Not significantly different)

columns.name: Upper 95CI RAR
columns.description: Upper 95% confidence interval around Risk Adjusted Rate

columns.name: Risk Adjusted Rate
columns.description:
The Risk Adjusted Rate (RAR), per 100 discharges: 
(Observed Rate / Expected Rate) * Statewide Rate

columns.name: Expected Rate
columns.description:
The Expected Mortality Rate, per 100 discharges:
(Expected Number of Deaths / Stroke Cases) * 100. 
Refer to Methods for definitions and risk adjustment model specifications.

columns.name: Observed Rate
columns.description:
The Observed Mortality Rate, per 100 discharges: 
(Died / Stroke Cases) * 100

description: This dataset includes income eligibility guidelines for participation in the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children (WIC). name: Healthy Neighborhoods Program, Housing Demographics and Conditions, Initial Visits and Revisits: Beginning 2006 - 2009 Funding Cycle
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Variable Detail
columns.description: Variable detail are the responses possible for each variables. Housing conditions details are Yes, No, Unknown, and Not Applicable. The details for each building characteristic or respondent demographics depends on the variable. For example, the details for Age of Building are Pre-1950, 1950-1978, and Post-1978.

columns.name: Frequency Count
columns.description: This is the number of dwellings that have a specific detail (e.g., the number of dwellings built before 1950, 1950-1978, after 1978, and Unknown). These sum to the number of dwellings visited.

columns.name: Variable
columns.description: Variables are the individual building characteristics, respondent demographics, or housing conditions assessed at the initial visit or revisit. Information about the building, primary respondent and residents are only collected at the initial visit.

columns.name: Visit Type
columns.description:
Types are Initial visits and Revisits.
At the initial visit an HNP surveyor gathers information about the building, primary respondent and residents and conducts an assessment of the conditions of the home. At this visit, the surveyor provides the HNP interventions and guidance to assist residents in addressing potential health and safety hazards. About 25% of these homes are revisited 3-6 months after the initial visit. Homes needing a revisit are prioritized on the overall or severity of the conditions identified at the initial visit or homes with residents with asthma. The home is reassessed for ongoing or new conditions and additional interventions are provided as needed.

columns.name: County Name
columns.description: The name of the county with a Healthy Neighborhoods Program (HNP). “All counties” is the combined data for all counties funded during a funding cycle.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The latitude and longitude of the county centroid.

columns.name: Funding Cycle
columns.description: County health departments submit applications to compete for funding to implement the Healthy Neighborhoods Program. Only full-service health departments are eligible to apply for funding. (A full-service health department has a Division of Nursing and a Division of Environmental Health.) Funding runs on cycles of 4 or 5 years. Both federal and state monies have funded the program; the current cycle is funded by the state budget.

columns.name: Item
columns.description: Variables are grouped into item types: housing information, respondent demographics, and housing conditions.

description: The NYS Healthy Neighborhoods Program (HNP) is a healthy homes program that seeks to reduce the burden of housing-related illness and injury. The dataset includes information about building characteristics, primary respondent demographics and the presence/absence of 34 specific housing conditions for dwellings visited by each county program.

name: Home Care Registry Training Data: Beginning 2009
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.description:
Total number of students in Withdrawn status.
Note: The status ‘Withdrawn’ describes those individuals who 
withdrew from the training class without the course completion.

columns.description: County where the Home Health Care Agency is located.

columns.name: City

columns.description: The street address of the Home Health Care Agency.

columns.description: Total number of students in Approved status.

columns.name: Enrolled Student Count
columns.description:
Total number of students in Enrolled status.
Note: The status ‘Enrolled’ describes those individuals who are currently registered in a training class.

columns.name: Methodology
columns.description:
There are six Methodologies defined in this dataset, out of which four are of type HHA and two are of type PCA. 
HHA Methodologies include: Core Training, Competency Evaluation, Nurse Aide Transition, and Personal Care Aide Upgrade. 
PCA Methodologies include: Basic Training, Alternative Competency Demonstration.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Represents the year in which number of training classes were provided by the Training Agency site.

columns.name: Certified Student Count
columns.description:
Total number of students in Certified status.
Note: An individual is considered certified when an authorized 
Official has signed and notarized the Certification form, attesting to 
the aide’s identity and his/her successful completion of the training 
program. Certifying a student is a multi-step process consisting of 
enrolling a student in a training class, approving the student’s 
successful completion of the class and then attesting to the student’s 
successful completion of the class.

columns.name: Language
columns.description: Language of the Program (Example: English, Spanish, Russian, etc.)

columns.name: Certificate Type
columns.description: Represents the type of Training Certificate. There are two certificate types defined in this dataset: Home Health Aide (HHA), Personal Care Aide (PCA).

columns.name: Operational Status
columns.description: The operational Status of the Training Agency site: Open or Closed.

columns.name: Approving State Agency
columns.description: The State Agency that has approved the Training Agency Site: New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH), New York State Education Department (NYSED).

columns.name: Number of Training Classes
columns.description:
Total number of training classes provided by the Training Agency Site
Note: The training classes count may be zero in a specific year, but the ‘Enrolled Student Count’ or ‘Approved Student Count’ or ‘Certified Student Count’ or ‘Withdrawn Student Count’ may be greater than Zero. This might be because a student enrolled in a specific year may not have been approved or certified or withdrawn in the same year.

description: The dataset provides training information extracted from Home Care Registry (HCR) application. The indicators are organized by the Priority Area of the Prevention Agenda as well as the Focus

name: Deer Tick Surveillance: Nymphs (May to Sept) excluding Powassan virus: Beginning 2008
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Total Sites Visited
columns.description: The total number of publicly accessible sites visited in the county. For the nymph dataset, this means publicly accessible sites visited from May to September.

columns.name: B. burgdorferi (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of nymph deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Borrelia burgdorferi, the bacteria that causes Lyme disease. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: Tick Population Density
columns.description:
The average number of nymph deer ticks (also known as blacklegged ticks or their scientific name Ixodes scapularis) collected per 1,000 meters sampled in the county. Tick population density is calculated only from the total nymph deer ticks collected (and does not include other species or life stages collected at the time of the site visit).  

Note: When taken in conjunction with percentage of nymph deer ticks positive (or proportion infected) with the specified bacteria or parasite, this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year. Statewide tick surveillance and testing began in 2008.

columns.name: A. phagocytophilum (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of nymph deer ticks that tested positive for Anaplasma phagocytophilum, the bacteria that causes anaplasmosis [or human granulocytic anaplasmosis (HGA)]. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or deer tick population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and the proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: Total Ticks Collected
columns.description: The total number of ticks (all species and life stages) collected in the county during visits to publicly accessible sites from May to September.

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county where ticks (all species and life stages) were collected. Ticks are often collected from multiple publicly accessible sites in each county.

columns.name: Total Tested
columns.description: Total number of nymph deer ticks that were tested for the listed bacteria or parasites. Ticks are tested individually.

columns.name: B. microti (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of nymph deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Babesia microti, the parasite that causes babesiosis. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year in which ticks (all species and life stages) were collected.

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county where ticks (all species and life stages) were collected. Ticks are often collected from multiple publicly accessible sites in each county.

columns.name: B. microti (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of adult deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Babesia microti, the parasite that causes babesiosis. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: Total Sites Visited
columns.description: The total number of publicly accessible sites visited in the county. For the adult dataset, this means publicly accessible sites visited from October to December.

columns.name: Total Ticks Collected
columns.description: The total number of ticks (all species and life stages) collected in the county during visits to publicly accessible sites from October to December.

columns.name: A. phagocytophilum (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of adult deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Anaplasma phagocytophilum, the bacteria that causes anaplasmosis [or human granulocytic anaplasmosis (HGA)]. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and the proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: B. burgdorferi (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of adult deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Borrelia burgdorferi, the bacteria that causes Lyme disease. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: Total Tested
columns.description: Total number of adult deer ticks that were tested for the listed bacteria or parasites. Ticks are tested individually.

columns.name: Tick Population Density
columns.description:
The average number of adult deer ticks (also known as blacklegged ticks or their scientific name Ixodes scapularis) collected per 1,000 meters sampled in the county. Tick population density is calculated only from the total adult deer ticks collected (and does not include other species or life stages collected at the time of the site visit).   

Note: When taken in conjunction with percentage of adult deer ticks positive (or proportion infected) with the specified bacteria or parasite, this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year. Statewide tick surveillance and testing began in 2008.

columns.name: County Centroid
columns.description: This is a centroid location within the county. It is used strictly for mapping purposes and does not reflect any specific place of the listed locality or county.

columns.name: B. miyamotoi (%)
columns.description:
Percentage of adult deer ticks that tested positive (or proportion infected) for Borrelia miyamotoi, the bacteria that can cause a relapsing-fever like disease with symptoms like those seen in anaplasmosis patients. 

Note: When taken in conjunction with tick population density (or population size), this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and proportion infected can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year. Testing of ticks for B. miyamotoi started in 2015.

description: This dataset includes eligibility criteria for participation in the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children (WIC). WIC offers nutrition education, breastfeeding support, referrals, and a variety of nutritious foods to low-income pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children up to age five to promote and support good health. To be eligible to receive WIC benefits, specific eligibility requirements must be met, including categorical, residential

name: Vital Statistics: Opioid-Related Deaths by Age Group: Beginning 2003

columns.description: Category of age at death in years, as documented on the death certificate.

columns.description: New York City (NYC) or Rest of State (ROS)

columns.name: Opioid Poisoning Deaths
columns.description: Death in which a drug poisoning is determined to be the underlying cause of death, or where underlying cause of death is missing or unknown from the first-listed multiple cause of death field and identified by ICD-10 codes X40- X44, X60-X64, X85, or Y10-Y14, with the involvement of opioids in the multiple cause of death (i.e., contributing causes documented on the death certificate), identified by ICD-10 codes T40.0 (opium), T40.1 (heroin), opioid analgesics (T40.2-T40.4), or other and unspecified narcotics (T40.6).

columns.name: Race or Ethnicity
columns.description: Decedent race (White, Black, Other) and ethnicity (Hispanic or non-Hispanic), documented on the death certificate.

columns.name: Sex
columns.description: Decedent sex as documented on the death certificate.

description: This dataset contains opioid-related deaths by age group. Opioid-related deaths include heroin and opioid analgesics mortalities.

name: Lead Testing in School Drinking Water Sampling and Results: Compliance Period 2023-2025
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.description: Number of outlets that may be used for drinking or cooking and require sampling

columns.name: Number of Outlets, Result ≤ 5 ppb
columns.description: Number of outlets with lead results less than or equal to 5 parts per billion (ppb) (or 5 µg/L or 0.005 mg/L). This reflects the most current lead results available. The lead results of initial sampling are reported here and replaced with post-remediation lead results, if performed.

columns.name: Number of Outlets, Result > 5 ppb
columns.description: Number of outlets with lead result(s) greater than 5 ppb (or 5 µg/L or 0.005 mg/L). This reflects the most current lead results available. The lead results of initial sampling are reported here and replaced with post-remediation lead results, if performed.

columns.name: Out of Service or Addressed
columns.description: Indicates if all outlets with lead results greater than 5 ppb (or 5 µg/L or 0.005 mg/L) have been taken out of service or addressed through remediation or controls.

description: This dataset shows the lead testing in school drinking water sampling and results information reported by each NYS public school and Boards of Cooperative Educational Services (BOCES) for Compliance Period 2023-2025. provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Date Year
columns.description: Year for the indicator rate or percent.

columns.description: Indicator value. Values are included for counties, and New York State. The cells are blank when data are not available, or suppressed.

columns.name: Trend Data NYC Value
columns.description: Indicator value. Values are for New York State, or are for New York City where the corresponding county is one of the NYC counties (Bronx, Kings, New York, Queens, Richmond), and blank otherwise.

columns.name: Health Topic
columns.description: Full title for the specific Health Topic area.

columns.name: Comment for Trend Data County Value
columns.description: Indicates whether the values in the county values column are for county or for New York State, and whether the data are suppressed or not available. If the cell in the comments column is “Not applicable”, the data are not available. If the cell in the comments column is “Data suppressed” there are insufficient observations and the value in the county value column is suppressed. If the cell in the comment column is blank, the value in the county value column is for county. If the cell in the comment column is “New York State” the value in the county value column is for New York State total.

columns.name: Indicator ID
columns.description: Unique indicator identifier

columns.name: Indicator Name
columns.description: Full text description of the indicator

columns.name: Data Source
columns.description: Data sources for the raw data used in indicator computation.

columns.name: Three Year Average County Value
columns.description: Indicator value of the county three–year average, where available. The cells are blank when data are not available, or suppressed due to insufficient number of observations.

columns.name: Trend Data NYS exc. NYC Value
columns.description: Indicator value. Values are for New York State, or are for New York State excluding New York City where the corresponding county is outside of New York City, and blank otherwise

columns.name: County Code
columns.description: County Code. New York State = 999.

columns.name: Health Topic Number
columns.description: Health Topic ID number

columns.name: County Name
columns.description: Full county name

columns.name: Comment for Three Year Average County Value
columns.description: If the cell in the comment column is blank, the value in the three year average county column is the three year average for the county: the average of: the year before, the current year and the year after. For county data, if the year is the first or last year in the trend, the three year average is not calculated, and the cell in the comment column is “Not applicable” Region values are not available for selected socio-economic indicators, including population, poverty and income, unemployment and health insurance status. Therefore, the cell in the comments column is “Not applicable”. The three year average are also not available when the county is New York State.

description: New York State Community Health Indicator Reports (CHIRS) were developed in 2012, and are regularly updated to consolidate and provide information regarding health indicators in the County Health Assessment Indicators (CHAI) for all communities in New York. The CHIRS present data for about 350 health indicators that are organized by 15 different health topics (see table below). Data tables are provided for all 62 New York State counties, 11 regions (including New York City), the State excluding

name: Birth Defect Prevalence: Beginning 1992
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Birth Year
columns.description: Year of child’s birth

columns.name: Birth Defect Name
columns.description: The name of the birth defect as defined by the National Birth Defects Prevention Network.

columns.name: Residence
columns.description: Maternal residence at time of birth as defined by county of residence or zip code of residence based on information from the birth certificate. “All” refers to all of New York State. “NYC” refers to New York City. “NYS excluding NYC” refers to the rest of NYS, except New York City. Based on mother’s residence at time of child’s birth from the birth certificate.

columns.name: Sex
columns.description: Sex of child: male or female

columns.name: Birth Defect Count
columns.description: Number of children with the indicated birth defect. A child can have more than one birth defect and may be counted under more than one birth defect category.

columns.name: Live Births
columns.description: The number of live births as reported by NYS vital statistics.

columns.name: Prevalence Per 10,000 Live Births
columns.description: Number of children with birth defects per 10,000 live births in the indicated time period and geographic location. (Number of children with the indicated birth defect/live births)*10,000 and rounded to the second decimal. “NA” indicates that the count of children with birth defects was zero, therefore prevalence is not applicable.

description: This dataset contains data from the New York State Birth Defects Registry (BDR). The BDR data presented shows the occurrence of selected major birth defects in children born in 1992 through 2020 who were matched with a birth certificate and had a mother who resided in NYS at the time of their birth. The data cover the entire state of New York, including New York City.

description: The Nursing Home Census historic data is the most comprehensive nursing home data available and is suitable for trending. Available bed and occupancy information is self-reported and is not audited by the Department of Health. The Department of Health requires nursing homes to complete electronic filing of each facility's licensed nursing home beds and availability by bed category on a weekly basis. This dataset is refreshed monthly.

name: Lead Testing in School Drinking Water Sampling and Results: Compliance Year 2020
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: County Location
columns.description: Latitude/longitude decimal degree coordinates for the region covered by the indicator, for use in mapping

columns.name: Date Survey Updated
columns.description: Date the data presented herein was last updated by the school

columns.name: School ZIP Code
columns.description: School zip code

columns.name: School Street
columns.description: School street

columns.name: BEDS Code
columns.description: Basic Education Data System (BEDS) code, the New York State Education Department identifier for NY schools

columns.name: Out of Service or Addressed
columns.description: Indicates if all outlets with lead results greater than 15 ppb (or 15 µg/L or 0.015 mg/L) have been taken out of service or addressed through remediation or controls. “Yes” = Outlets with lead greater than 15 ppb are removed from service, remediated, or have appropriate controls;  “No” = Some outlets with lead greater than 15 ppb remain in services and appropriate controls are not in place; or “N/A” = Not applicable, there are no outlets with lead greater than 15 ppb or the entire school is lead-free and no samples were collected

columns.name: Number of Outlets, Result > 15 ppb
columns.description: Number of outlets with lead result(s) greater than 15 ppb (or 15 µg/L or 0.015 mg/L). This reflects the most current lead results available. The lead results of initial sampling are reported here and replaced with post-remediation lead results, if performed.

columns.name: Sampling Completion Date
columns.description: Date all initial sampling was completed for Compliance Year 2020 (not including post remediation sampling)

columns.name: Type of Organization
columns.description: Public school or Board of Cooperative Educational Services (BOCES)

columns.name: County
columns.description: County where the school is located

columns.name: Number of Outlets that Require Sampling
columns.description: Number of outlets that may be used for drinking or cooking and require sampling

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Latitude and longitude

columns.name: School State
columns.description: School state

columns.name: All Results Received
columns.description: Indicates if all lead results for the initial sampling for Compliance Year 2020 were received (not including post-remediation sampling results). “Yes” = Received, “No” = Awaiting test results, or “N/A” = Not applicable, the entire school is lead-free and no samples were collected

columns.name: Compliance Year
columns.description: Compliance year

columns.name: School District
columns.description: School district name

columns.name: Date All Results Received
columns.description: Date all lead results were received for the initial sampling for Compliance Year 2020 (not including post-remediation sampling results)

columns.name: Number of Outlets, Result ≤ 15 ppb
columns.description: Number of outlets with lead results less than or equal to 15 parts per billion (ppb) (or 15 µg/L or 0.015 mg/L). This reflects the most current lead results available. The lead results of initial sampling are reported here and replaced with post-remediation lead results, if performed.

columns.name: Remediation Status
columns.description: Provides the status of remediation. Examples of remediation include but are not limited to: permanent removal of outlets; replacing outlets and/or plumbing; or employing other engineering controls.  “Not Started” = One or more outlets exceed 15 ppb and remediation is planned; “On-going” = One or more outlets exceed 15 ppb and remediation is underway but incomplete; “Complete” = Remediation was performed and is complete; “N/A” = Not applicable, there are no outlets exceeding 15 ppb or the entire school is lead-free and no samples were collected

columns.name: School Website
columns.description: The website where the lead results are posted

columns.name: School
columns.description: School name

columns.name: Any Building with Lead-Free Plumbing?
columns.description: “Yes (Entire Building)” = Entire school has Lead-Free plumbing and does not require sampling; “Yes (Partial Building)” = portion of school has Lead-Free plumbing; or “No” = the school does not have Lead-Free plumbing

columns.name: Sampling Complete
columns.description: Indicates if all initial sampling for the school for Compliance Year 2020 is complete. “Yes” = Complete; “No” = Incomplete; or “N/A” = Not applicable, entire school is lead-free and no samples were collected

columns.name: School City
columns.description: School city

description: This dataset shows the lead testing in school drinking water sampling and results information reported by each NYS public school and Boards of Cooperative Educational Services (BOCES) for Compliance Year 2020. Public Health Law (Section 110) and New York State (NYS) Department of Health regulation (10 NYCRR 67-4) mandate that NYS public school districts and BOCES test drinking water for lead contamination and report the results to parents, the NYS Department of Health, NY State Education Departm

This column provides the activity number associated with the
intervention identified.

columns.name: Location 1
columns.description: Latitude and longitude for the location (or best approximation) of where the intervention occurred.

columns.name: Activity
columns.description:
Activities represent the five broad groups that interventions are
grouped into. Activity areas 1 and 2 include interventions that
address opportunities for physical activity, and Activity areas 3 and 4
include interventions that address improving nutrition. Activity area 5
represents interventions in child care and after school sites that
address both physical activity and nutrition. A detailed description of
these activity areas can be found in the Dataset Overview document.

columns.name: Intervention
columns.description:
Interventions represent one of 16 possible policy, system or
environmental approaches chosen by Creating Healthy Places
contractors that are identified in the literature as promising or
evidence based for improving nutrition or increasing opportunities for
physical activity at the community level. A full list of the possible
interventions can be found in the Dataset Overview document.

columns.name: Zip code
columns.description: The six digit postal code where the intervention is located.

name: New York State Statewide COVID-19 Wastewater Surveillance Data
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: County
columns.description: Name of the county from where the sample is collected.

columns.name: ZIPCode
columns.description: ZIP code in which this sampling site is located.

columns.name: PopulationServed
columns.description: Estimated number of persons served by this sampling site (i.e., served by this wastewater treatment plant or, if 'SampleLocation' is "upstream", then by this upstream location).

columns.name: SampleLocation
columns.description: Sample collection location in the wastewater system, whether at a wastewater treatment plant (or other community level treatment infrastructure such as community-scale septic) or upstream in the wastewater system.

columns.name: SampleLocationSpecify
columns.description: If 'SampleLocation' is "upstream", we may specify the collection location in the wastewater system.

columns.name: EPAID
columns.description: NPDES permit number for the wastewater treatment plant specified in 'WWTPName.'

columns.name: WWTPName
columns.description: The name of the Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) to which this wastewater flows. If this wastewater does not flow to a WWTP, we specify an identifiable name for the septic or other treatment system to which this wastewater flows.

columns.name: CapacityMGD
columns.description: Wastewater treatment plant design capacity. This should be the capacity for which the plant is permitted.

columns.name: StormwaterInput
columns.description: Does the wastewater treatment plant specified in 'WWTPName' treat water from a combined sewer system (i.e., a sewer system that collects both sewage and stormwater).

columns.name: SampleType
columns.description: Type of sample collected, whether grab or composite. If composite, also provide the duration of sampling and type of composite, as listed in the Value Set (e.g., "24-hr flow-weighted composite"). A grab sample is defined as an individual sample collected without compositing or adding other samples, regardless of whether the sample matrix is liquid wastewater or sludge.

columns.name: SampleMatrix
columns.description: Wastewater matrix from which the sample was collected.

columns.name: Pretreatment
columns.description: Was the sample treated with any chemicals prior to reaching the lab? These could include chemicals, such as stabilizers, added to the sample or chemicals, such as chlorine, added to the wastewater treatment train upstream of the sample collection point. Pasteurization should be specified in the 'pasteurized' field.

columns.name: SolidSeparation
columns.description: Process used to separate solid and liquid phases of the sample, either prior to or in the absence of the concentration method specified in 'ConcentrationMethod.'

columns.name: ConcentrationMethod
columns.description: Method used to concentrate the sample prior to analysis of the concentrate.

columns.name: ExtractionMethod
columns.description: Method used for nucleic acid extraction from the sample.

columns.name: RecEffTargetName
columns.description: Name of the recovery efficiency control target that is spiked in.

columns.name: RecEffSpikeMatrix
columns.description: Matrix into which the recovery efficiency control target is spiked.

columns.name: RecEffSpikeConc
columns.description: Spike concentration, on average, of the recovery control on a per sample volume basis.

columns.name: PCRTarget
columns.description: The target of the PCR quantification.

columns.name: PCRGeneTarget
columns.description: The PCR gene used to quantify PCR target.

columns.name: PCRGeneTargetRef
columns.description: A publication, website, or brief description of the PCR gene target used.

columns.name: PCRType
columns.description: The type of PCR used to quantify the PCR target.

columns.name: LodRef
columns.description: A publication, website, or brief description of the method used to calculate the limit of detection.

columns.name: HumFracTargetMic
columns.description: Name of microbial target used to estimate human fecal content.

columns.name: HumFracTargetMicRef
columns.description: A publication, website, or brief description of the microbial target specified in 'HumFracTargetMic.'

columns.name: QuantStanType
columns.description: The type of nucleic acid used as a standard for the PCR target quantification.

columns.name: StanRef
columns.description: A publication, website, or brief description of the quantitative standard material used.

columns.name: InhibitionMethod
columns.description: A publication, website, or brief description of the method used to evaluate molecular inhibition.

columns.name: NumNoTargetControl
columns.description: Number of no-template controls (NTC) per instrument run.

columns.name: SampleCollectDate
columns.description: The date of sample collection; for composite samples, we specify the date on which sample collection began.

columns.name: FlowRate
columns.description: Wastewater volumetric flow rate at the sample collection location over the 24-hr period during which the sample was collected. If only an instantaneous flow measurement is available, it may be reported in units of million gallons per day.

columns.name: SampleID
columns.description: Unique ID assigned to a wastewater sample.

columns.name: LabID
columns.description: Unique ID assigned to a testing lab.

columns.name: TestResultDate
columns.description: The date on which this PCR target measurement was made.

columns.name: PCRTargetAvgConc
columns.description: Concentration of the PCR target back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis; "0" if no amplification occurred, using the definition of amplification described in 'NtcAmplify'; otherwise, it is the estimated concentration (copies/L wastewater).

columns.name: PCRTargetBelowLod
columns.description: Was the concentration of the PCR target below the limit of detection?

columns.name: LodSewage
columns.description: PCR target limit of detection back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis.

columns.name: NtcAmplify
columns.description: For qPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have a Ct value less than 40? For ddPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have 3 or more positive droplets?

columns.name: RecEffPercent
columns.description: Percent of spiked recovery control, specified in 'RecEffTargetName', that was recovered.

columns.name: InhibitionDetect
columns.description: Was molecular inhibition detected?

columns.name: InhibitionAdjust
columns.description: Was inhibition incorporated into the PCR target concentration calculation?

columns.name: HumFracMicConc

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid review, and the CAHPS survey.  Administrative review utilizes administrative systems only to identify the eligible population, numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate.  Hybrid review utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible population in addition to medical chart review.  The CAHPS survey is an annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Domain

Information from the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) consumer satisfaction survey is included in the Provider Network, Adults Living with Illness, as well as in the Satisfaction with Care sections.

name: Deer Tick Surveillance: Nymphs (May to Sept) Powassan Virus Only: Beginning 2009
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year in which ticks (all species and life stages) were collected.

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county where ticks (all species and life stages) were collected. Ticks are often collected from multiple publicly accessible sites in each county.

columns.name: Total Sites Visited
columns.description: The total number of publicly accessible sites visited in the county. For the nymph dataset, this means publicly accessible sites visited from May to September.

columns.name: Total Ticks Collected
columns.description: The total number of ticks (all species and life stages) collected in the county during visits to publicly accessible sites from May to September.

columns.name: Tick Population Density
columns.description:
The average number of nymph deer ticks (also known as blacklegged ticks or their scientific name Ixodes scapularis) collected per 1,000 meters sampled in the county. Tick population density is calculated only from the total nymph deer ticks collected (and does not include other species or life stages collected at the time of the site visit).   

Note: When taken in conjunction with Minimum Infection Rate (MIR) for Powassan virus, this field can give a sense of risk of encountering an infected tick. But tick population density and MIR can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year. Statewide testing of ticks for Powassan virus began in 2009.

columns.name: Pools Tested
columns.description:
The number of pools of nymph deer ticks tested for Powassan virus. Typically, 5-10 ticks are processed together in one tube and tested as one unit (called a “pool”).

Note: Testing is for the Powassan group of viruses, which includes Powassan virus and Deer Tick virus.  Because of the low expected infection rate of Powassan virus in deer ticks, they are not tested individually, but in pools.

columns.name: Pools Positive
columns.description:
Total number of nymph deer tick pools that tested positive for Powassan virus. 

Note: This testing will detect both Powassan virus and Deer Tick virus, but does not differentiate between the two. It is expected that the vast majority of nymph deer ticks testing positive are infected with Deer Tick virus.

columns.name: Minimum Infection Rate
columns.description:
When tick pools test positive, it is assumed that one tick in the pool was positive. Minimum Infection Rate (MIR) is the measure used to address this. MIR is the ratio of the number of positive pools to the total number of nymph ticks tested. 

Note: This field can give a sense of ticks infected with Powassan virus in a particular area.  But tick population density and (MIR) can vary widely at different publicly accessible sites within a county, as well as from year to year.

columns.name: County Location
columns.description: This is a centroid location within the county. It is used strictly for mapping purposes and does not reflect any specific place of the listed locality or county..

columns.name: Total Ticks Tested
columns.description: Total number of nymph deer ticks that were tested for Powassan virus.

description: This dataset provides the results from collecting and testing nymph deer ticks, also known as blacklegged ticks, or by their scientific name Ixodes scapularis. Collection and testing take place across New York State (excluding New York City) from May to September, when nymph deer ticks are most commonly seen. Nymph deer ticks are tested in “pools”, or groups of up to ten adult ticks per pool, for the Powassan virus, also known as Deer tick virus. These data should simply be used to educate peopl

columns.description: Physical form of drug; Medi-span reference file.

columns.description: Name of pharmaceutical company corresponding to the first segment of National Drug Code (NDC); 'NA' if unavailable; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.description: Classification of drug based on conditions treated or effect on the body.

columns.description: Categorization of drug as brand or generic; 'NA' if unavailable.

columns.description: Proprietary or trade name; Name of the drug chosen by the labeler; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: Nonproprietary Drug Name
columns.description: Nonproprietary or generic name of drug; Typically the active ingredient; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: NDC 9 digits
columns.description: First two segments of National Drug Code standardized in 5-4 format with leading zeros where necessary and no hyphen (9 digits total).

columns.name: Year
columns.description: 4-digit Year in which prescriptions were filled.

columns.description: Standard deviation of the amounts paid by the member's health plan to provider across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Active Strength Unit
columns.description: Assigned unit to be used with active strength value; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: File Type
columns.description: File type ('NDC' in this public use file).

columns.description: Sum of the amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Total Paid Amount
columns.description: Standard Deviation of the total amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.description: Mean of the total amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Sum Member Paid Amount
columns.description: Sum of the amounts paid by the member, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Member Paid Amount
columns.description: Standard Deviation of the amounts paid by the member to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Mean Member Paid Amount
columns.description: Mean of the amounts paid by the member to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Median Member Paid Amount
columns.description: Median of the amounts paid by the member to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Sum Insurer Paid Amount
columns.description: Sum of the amounts paid by the member's health plan to provider across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.description: Median of the amounts paid by the member's health plan to provider across all prescriptions in the public use file.

description: This dataset is designed to analyze prescription drug use and spending among New York State residents at the drug product level (pharmacy claims data that have been aggregated by labeler code and product code segments of the National Drug Code). The dataset includes the number of prescriptions filled by unique members by payer type, nonproprietary name, labeler name, dosage characteristics, amount insurer paid, and more.

name: Managed Long-Term Care Regional Consumer Guide
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: CG Area
columns.description:
Consumer guide areas are comprised of one or more domains.  The areas include:
• Preventive Care
• Quality of Life
• Satisfaction with Care
• Stability or Improvement
• Overall

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Total Stars
columns.description: The standard deviation of all plan total stars.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable domain scores and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Total Stars
columns.description: A plan’s total stars is the sum of all the domain ratings (stars) for that plan.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable domain scores and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Measure Category
columns.description: Represents the category (satisfaction or quality) that each measure belongs to.

columns.name: Domain Rating
columns.description: A health plan’s rating for an area, domain, or overall.  N/A means not able to report due to small numbers, or plan performance data are not available.

columns.name: Plan Name
columns.description: Name of the MLTC plan certified by NYSDOH to enroll Medicaid recipients.

columns.name: Critical Value
columns.description: The critical value associated with each measure based on the method of collection for that measure.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable results for that measure and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Plan ID
columns.description: A unique identification number for each plan.

columns.name: Average of Total Stars
columns.description: The mean of all plan total stars.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable domain scores and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Measure Type
columns.description: Type of statistic.

columns.name: Average of Domain Scores
columns.description: The mean of all plan scores for that domain.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable domain scores and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Member Services Phone Numbers
columns.description: Plan specific member services phone number during measurement year.

columns.name: Average of Critical Values
columns.description: Average of the critical values for all measures in a domain.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable measures for a domain and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: CG Domain
columns.description:
Consumer guide domains are comprised of one or more measures.  The domains include:
• ADL Stable or Improved
• Advanced Directives
• Pain Intensity Stable or Improved
• Patient Safety
• Prevention
• Quality of Life
• Rating of Care Manager
• Rating of Health Plan
• Rating of Regular Visiting Nurse
• Shortness of Breath Stable or Improved
• Timeliness of Aide
• Urinary Continence Stable or Improved
• Overall

columns.name: Region
columns.description: New York State region where health plan operated during the measurement year.  N/A means the plan was no longer available in a region at the time of dataset creation.

columns.name: Program Type
columns.description: Indicates MLTC plan model.

columns.name: Measurement Period
columns.description: Represents the period during which the enrollees are assessed.

columns.name: Measurement Year
columns.description: Represents the evaluation year.

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Domain Scores
columns.description: The standard deviation of all plan scores for that domain. The value is missing for plans that had no reportable domain scores and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Measure Name
columns.description: Name of MLTC measure. (See the Managed Long-Term Care Performance Data: Beginning in 2014 Measures documentation, available on Health Data NY for further details about MLTC measures.)

columns.name: Domain Score
columns.description: A plan’s domain score is the average of the standardized measure scores within the domain for that plan.  The value is missing for plans that had no reportable measures for a domain and for the “Overall” Measure Name.

columns.name: Web Sites
columns.description: Plan specific web site address during measurement year.

description: The Managed Long-Term Care (MLTC) consumer guide data provides information about the quality of care offered by the different plans and people’s opinions about the care and the services the plans provide. This information will help consumers choose a managed long-term care plan that meets their health care needs. The Managed Long-Term Care Regional Consumer Guide dataset contains each plan’s current ratings, the regions of the state they serve, customer service telephone number, and website.

name: Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) Health Disparities 2019
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, Commercial PPO.

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid review, and the CAHPS survey. Administrative review utilizes administrative systems only to identify the eligible population, numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate. Hybrid review utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible population in addition to medical chart review. The CAHPS survey is an annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: This field reflects characteristics that are extracted from the member information that is collected during enrollment and is linked to QARR member-level data. The categories of demographic information include: members’ gender, age, race/ethnicity, Medicaid aid category, payer status, cash assistance status, mental health condition, substance use, and region of residence.

columns.name: Characteristics
columns.description: This field reflects the range of values that are appropriate for each demographic category.

columns.name: Numerator
columns.description: Number of persons within each characteristic.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting denominator criteria specified by HEDIS 2019 within each characteristic.

columns.name: Rate
columns.description: Percentage of numerator compliant within the denominator criteria.

columns.name: Sub Domain
columns.description: Category within the domain with more detailed information regarding a certain aspect of quality.

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: The measures in QARR are divided into seven domains: Provider, Network, Access to Primary Care, Child and Adolescent Health, Women’s Health, Adult Health, Behavioral Health, and Satisfaction with Care. Information from the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) consumer satisfaction survey is included in the Provider Network, Adults Living with Illness, as well as in the Satisfaction with Care sections.

columns.name: Measurement Year
columns.description: The time period the data represent.

columns.name: Measure
columns.description: Measure short name that corresponds to the short name in the QARR dataset file.

columns.name: Measure Description
columns.description: Description of quality measure. This dataset contains 66 measures from the QARR.

description: This dataset includes Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, and Commercial PPO performance data from the Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) by member demographic characteristics. QARR is largely based on measures of quality developed and published by the National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS®). Plans are required to submit quality performance data each year. Demographic information analyzed in this report includes m

columns.description: Median of the amounts paid by the member's health plan to provider across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Standard Deviation Quantity Dispensed

columns.name: Mean Quantity Dispensed

columns.name: Median Quantity Dispensed
columns.description: Median quantity of dosage form dispensed with prescription across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Standard Deviation Days Supply
columns.description: Standard deviation of days supply across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Median Days Supply

columns.name: Unique Members

columns.name: Active Strength Unit
columns.description: Assigned unit to be used with active strength value; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: Active Strength
columns.description: Quantity of the active ingredient(s) included in each tablet or other dosage form; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: Dosage Form
columns.description: Physical form of drug; Medi-span reference file.

columns.name: Labeler Name
columns.description: Name of pharmaceutical company corresponding to the first segment of National Drug Code (NDC); 'NA' if unavailable; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: Therapeutic Class
columns.description: Classification of drug based on conditions treated or effect on the body.

columns.name: Proprietary Drug Name
columns.description: Proprietary or trade name; Name of the drug chosen by the labeler; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: Nonproprietary Drug Name
columns.description: Nonproprietary or generic name of drug; Typically the active ingredient; Medi-Span reference file.

columns.name: NDC 9 digits
columns.description: First two segments of National Drug Code standardized in 5-4 format with leading zeros where necessary and no hyphen (9 digits total).

columns.name: Number of Prescriptions Filled
columns.description: Number of prescriptions filled aggregated in this public use file.

columns.name: Sum Member Paid Amount
columns.description: Sum of the amounts paid by the member, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Median Total Paid Amount
columns.description: Median of the total amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file,

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Insurer Paid Amount
columns.description: Standard deviation of the amounts paid by the member's health plan to provider across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: 4-digit Year in which prescriptions were filled.

columns.name: Drug Category
columns.description: Categorization of drug as brand or generic; 'NA' if unavailable.

columns.name: Payer Type
columns.description: Categorization of payer reported on pharmacy claims: Commercial, Medicare, New York Programs.

columns.name: Mean Days Supply
columns.description: Mean days supply across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Sum Total Paid Amount
columns.description: Sum of the amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Standard Deviation of Total Paid Amount
columns.description: Standard Deviation of the total amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

columns.name: Mean Total Paid Amount
columns.description: Mean of the total amounts paid by the member and the member’s health plan to provider when not zero, as reported by the health plan, across all prescriptions in the public use file.

description: This dataset is designed to analyze prescription drug use and spending among New York State residents at the drug product level (pharmacy claims data that have been aggregated by labeler code and product code segments of the National Drug Code). The dataset includes the number of prescriptions filled by unique members by payer type, nonproprietary name, labeler name, dosage characteristics, amount insurer paid, and more.

description: The Individual Provider Network Data displays information on individuals participating in health plan networks from January through March 2020. Plan network data is collected from Medicaid, Commercial, and Exchange plans on a quarterly basis by the Department of Health, including managed care plans, as well as PPO/EPO plans. For more information, please visit https://pndslookup.health.ny.gov.
