description: Effective July 14, 2009, physician offices that perform surgical or invasive procedures using more than mild sedation must be accredited by one of three accrediting agencies. The NYSDOH collects OBS practice site accreditation data from each of the accrediting agencies monthly. The OBS practice accreditation site data is used to keep an accurate list of accredited OBS practice sites for patient safety and regulatory efforts. For more information, visit https://www.health.ny.gov/professionals/off

name: Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) Health Disparities 2018
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid review, and the CAHPS survey. Administrative review utilizes administrative systems only to identify the eligible population, numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate. Hybrid review utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible population in addition to medical chart review. The CAHPS survey is an annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, Commercial PPO.

columns.name: Rate
columns.description: Percentage of numerator compliant within the denominator criteria.

columns.name: Characteristics
columns.description: This field reflects the range of values that are appropriate for each demographic category.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting denominator criteria specified by HEDIS 2019 within each characteristic.

columns.name: Sub Domain
columns.description: Category within the domain with more detailed information regarding a certain aspect of quality.

columns.name: Numerator
columns.description: Number of persons within each characteristic.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: This field reflects characteristics that are extracted from the member information that is collected during enrollment and is linked to QARR member-level data. The categories of demographic information include: members’ gender, age, race/ethnicity, Medicaid aid category, payer status, cash assistance status, mental health condition, substance use, and region of residence.

columns.name: Measure Description
columns.description: Description of quality measure.  This dataset contains 66 measures from the QARR.

columns.name: Measurement Year
columns.description: The time period the data represent.

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: The measures in QARR are divided into seven domains: Provider, Network, Access to Primary Care, Child and Adolescent Health, Women’s Health, Adult Health, Behavioral Health, and Satisfaction with Care. Information from the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) consumer satisfaction survey is included in the Provider Network, Adults Living with Illness, as well as in the Satisfaction with Care sections.

columns.name: Measure
columns.description: Measure short name that corresponds to the short name in the QARR dataset file.

description: This dataset includes Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, and Commercial PPO performance data from the Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) by member demographic characteristics. QARR is largely based on measures of quality developed and published by the National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS®). Plans are required to submit quality performance data each year. Demographic information analyzed in this report includes m

provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Expected Rate Per 100,000 People
columns.description: The expected number of PQI discharges in the county, adjusted by age, gender and race/ethnicity divided by the county population, multiplied by 100,000.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year of both the hospital PQI discharge and the county population used in calculation of rates.

columns.name: Software Version
columns.description: AHRQ software version used to produce quality indicators for this year.

columns.name: PQI Number
columns.description: The value code assigned to the PQI measure.

columns.name: PQI Name
columns.description: The descriptive name of the PQI measure.

columns.name: Patient County
columns.description: The patient’s county of residence at time of discharge. Hospital discharges that did not have an accurate New York State county were excluded.

columns.name: Risk Adjusted Rate per 100,000 People
columns.description: The observed PQI rate divided by the expected PQI rate, multiplied by the statewide PQI rate.

columns.name: Difference in Rates
columns.description: Observed rate minus expected rate.

columns.name: Observed Rate Per 100,000 People
columns.description: The number of PQI discharges in the county divided by the county population, multiplied by 100,000.

columns.description: The observed prescribing rate divided by the expected prescribing rate, multiplied by the statewide prescribing rate.

columns.description: The number of eligible index visits in the provider county for which an antibiotic prescription was filled.

columns.name: Observed Prescribing Rate per 100 Visits
columns.description: The number of eligible index visits in the provider county for which an antibiotic prescription was filled divided by the total number of eligible index visits in the provider county, multiplied by 100.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year of the eligible index visits.

columns.name: Provider County
columns.description: The provider county where the visit occurred.  Visits that did not occur within New York State are excluded.

columns.name: Index Visits
columns.description: The number of initial visits for Acute Respiratory Infection (ARI) that met inclusion criteria in the provider county.

description: This dataset contains Potentially Avoidable Antibiotic Prescribing observed and risk-adjusted rates for child Medicaid enrollees by provider county beginning in 2010. description: This dataset represents hospital-reported data for all pediatric patients with a diagnosis of severe sepsis or septic shock seen at New York State Article 28 (acute care) facilities and reported to the New York State Department of Health. Quality measures are calculated using this data for reporting hospital- specific rates and trends. These measures, reported as statewide and hospital-specific rates, reflect several key processes of care that can increase the probability of surviving an episode

name: Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) Health Disparities 2020
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Sub Domain
columns.description: Category within the domain with more detailed information regarding a certain aspect of quality.

columns.name: Mmeasurement Year
columns.description: The time period the data represent.

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid review, and the CAHPS survey. Administrative review utilizes administrative systems only to identify the eligible population, numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate. Hybrid review utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible population in addition to medical chart review. The CAHPS survey is an annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Numerator
columns.description: Number of persons within each characteristic.

columns.name: Rate
columns.description: Percentage of numerator compliant within the denominator criteria.

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, Commercial PPO.

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: The measures in QARR are divided into seven domains: Provider, Network, Access to Primary Care, Child and Adolescent Health, Women’s Health, Adult Health, Behavioral Health, and Satisfaction with Care. Information from the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) consumer satisfaction survey is included in the Provider Network, Adults Living with Illness, as well as in the Satisfaction with Care sections.

columns.name: Measure Description
columns.description: Description of quality measure. This dataset contains 66 measures from the QARR.

columns.name: Measure

columns.name: Category

columns.name: Characteristics
columns.description: This field reflects the range of values that are appropriate for each demographic category.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting denominator criteria specified by HEDIS 2019 within each characteristic.

description: This dataset includes Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, and Commercial PPO performance data from the Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) by member demographic characteristics. CHHAs/LTHHCPs provide part time, intermittent, skilled services which are of a preventative, therapeutic, rehabilitative, health guidance and/or supportive nature to persons at home. Home health services include nursing services; home health aide services; medical supplies, equipment, and appliances suitable for use in the home; and at leas

name: School Immunization Survey:  Beginning 2019-20 School Year
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Percent Immunized Mumps
columns.description: Percent of students that have met the proof of immunity requirements for mumps

columns.name: District
columns.description: Name of school district

columns.name: Percent Immunized Tdap
columns.description: Percent of students that have met the proof of immunity requirements for tdap

columns.name: Type
columns.description: School district (type 3), public (type 1), private school (type 2) or  Board of Educational Cooperative Services (BOCES - type 4)

columns.name: Percent Completely Immunized
columns.description: Percent of students that have met the proof of immunity  requirements for each antigen (diphtheria, polio, measles, mumps,  rubella, hepatitis B and varicella)

columns.name: State
columns.description: State where the school is located.

columns.name: NYS Tick Regions
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'NYS Tick Regions' (v9ry-wcha) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: School Districts 2016.shp
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'School Districts 2016.shp' (5edz-4hdv) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Counties
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Counties' (9yqb-tdyd) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: ComputedFieldforMapping1
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'ComputedFieldforMapping1' (assa-msit) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: New York Zip Codes
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'New York Zip Codes' (43an-4dx5) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: County
columns.description: County of the address. The address listed for the schools may be  the physical location of the school, a school district address, or an  administrative address.

columns.name: Street
columns.description: The street address. The address listed for the schools may be the  physical location of the school, a school district address, or an  administrative address.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city of the address. The address listed for the schools may be  the physical location of the school, a school district address, or an  administrative address.

columns.description: Percent of students that have met the proof of immunity requirements for measles

columns.description: Percent of students that have met the proof of immunity requirements for polio

description: The School Immunization Survey collects aggregate data from schools in New York State regarding the immunization status of all students attending school. All schools, excluding New York City public schools, have reported the immunization status of all students in grades kindergarten through 12.

name: Hospital Inpatient Discharges (SPARCS De-Identified): Maternal Sepsis by County and Demographics: 2016-2018
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Any Sepsis Events During Post-Partum (N)
columns.description: The number of all maternal sepsis events (including severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding within 42 days following delivery for eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Any Sepsis Events During Post-Partum (per 100,000)
columns.description: The rate of all maternal sepsis events (including severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding within 42 days following delivery per 100,000 eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Severe Sepsis/ Septic Shock During Post-Partum (N)
columns.description: The number of maternal severe sepsis/septic shock events identified via diagnosis coding within 42 days following delivery for eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name:
Severe Sepsis/ Septic Shock During Post-Partum
(per 100,000)
columns.description: The rate of maternal severe sepsis/septic shock events identified via diagnosis coding within 42 days following delivery per 100,000 eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name:
Severe Sepsis/ Septic Shock During Delivery
(per 100,000)
columns.description: The rate of maternal severe sepsis/septic shock events identified via diagnosis coding on the delivery claim per 100,000 eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Year(s) of Live Birth
columns.description: The years during which the live births represented in the data occurred.

columns.name: Any Sepsis Events Peripartum (N)
columns.description: The number of all maternal sepsis events (including severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding at any point between the beginning of pregnancy through 42 days post-delivery among eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata.  A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Any Sepsis Events During Pregnancy (N)
columns.description: The number of all maternal sepsis events (including severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding during pregnancy among eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Severe Sepsis/ Septic Shock During Pregnancy (N)

A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Any Sepsis Events During Delivery (N)
columns.description: The number of all maternal sepsis events (including severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding on the delivery claim for eligible live births within the specified patient county and demographic strata. A value of ‘SS’ indicates redacted information due to de-identification criteria.

columns.name: Patient County
columns.description: The patient county of residence reported during the delivery event.  Women residing outside of NYS are excluded.

columns.name: Demographic Characteristics

description: The dataset contains administratively identified maternal sepsis observed event counts and rates related to live births during the pregnancy, delivery, and postpartum windows by patient county and demographics between 2016 and 2018. Maternal sepsis is a leading cause of maternal mortality in the United States and is associated with increased rates of preterm labor, preterm delivery and fetal infection and maternal chronic pain and fertility problems. Live births were identified from administrati

name: Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) Health Disparities 2021
provider: New York State Department

columns.name: Rate
columns.description: Percentage of numerator compliant within the denominator criteria.

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid review, and the CAHPS survey. Administrative review utilizes administrative systems only to identify the eligible population, numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate. Hybrid review utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible population in addition to medical chart review. The CAHPS survey is an annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Measure
columns.description: Measure short name that corresponds to the short name in the QARR dataset file.

columns.name: Sub Domain
columns.description: Category within the domain with more detailed information regarding a certain aspect of quality.

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: The measures in QARR are divided into seven domains: Provider, Network, Access to Primary Care, Child and Adolescent Health, Women’s Health, Adult Health, Behavioral Health, and Satisfaction with Care.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting denominator criteria specified by HEDIS   within each characteristic.

columns.name: Measurement Year
columns.description: The time period the data represent.

columns.name: Measure Description
columns.description: Description of quality measure.

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, Commercial PPO.

columns.name: Numerator
columns.description: Number of persons within each characteristic.

columns.name: Characteristics
columns.description: This field reflects the range of values that are appropriate for each demographic category.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: This field reflects characteristics that are extracted from the member information that is collected during enrollment and is linked to QARR member-level data. The categories of demographic information include: members’ sex, age, race, Medicaid aid category, payer status, cash assistance status, mental health condition, substance use, and region of residence.

description: This dataset includes Medicaid Managed Care, Commercial HMO, and Commercial PPO performance data from the Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) by member demographic characteristics. QARR is largely based on measures of quality developed and published by the National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS®). Plans are required to submit quality performance data each year. Demographic information analyzed in this report includes m

description: The Institutional Provider Network Data displays information on health facilities and ancillary service providers (for example: hospitals, labs, home care agencies) participating in health plan networks from October through December 2020. Plan network data is collected from Medicaid, Commercial, and Exchange plans on a quarterly basis by NYSoH, including managed care plans, as well as PPO/EPO plans. For more information, please visit: https://pndslookup.health.ny.gov.

tags: new york state donate life registry
description: This dataset contains monthly snapshots of the number of enrollments by year of birth in the New York State Donate Life Registry beginning in September 2008. The registry is a confidential database that records a person’s consent to organ/tissue donation upon death. The database is used by donation professionals to determine a person's donation status at the time of their death. For more information, please visit https://donatelife.ny.gov/.

name: Hospital Emergency Department Discharges (SPARCS De-Identified): Emergency Department Encounters by Facility: Beginning 2018
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Facility Name
columns.description: The name of the facility where services were provided based on the Facility Identifier, as maintained by the NYSDOH Division of Health Facility Planning.

columns.name: ED Encounters Admitted as Inpatient
columns.description: The number of ED encounters at the facility that resulted in patient being admitted to the facility. Redacted values are represented with an ‘S’.

columns.name: Facility Region
columns.description: Region in which the facility is located (New York City, Outside of New York City).

columns.name: Facility County
columns.description: The name of the county in which the facility is located.

columns.name: Operating Certificate Number
columns.description: Operating Certificate number for the facility.

columns.name: Operator Name
columns.description: Name of the operator of the facility.

columns.name: Total ED Encounters
columns.description: The total number of ED encounters at the facility, including ED treat and release, ED encounters requiring ambulatory surgery, and ED encounters that resulted in an inpatient admission. Redacted values are represented with an ‘S’.

columns.name: Treat and Release ED Encounters
columns.description: The number of ED encounters at the facility when a patient was released without requiring ambulatory surgery or being admitted to the facility. Redacted values are represented with an ‘S’.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Indicates the year of the Emergency Department (ED) encounter;  derived from the date the patient was admitted at the facility.

columns.name: Quarter
columns.description: Indicates the calendar quarter of the ED encounter.

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description: Site-specific Permanent Facility Identifier (PFI).

columns.name: Facility County FIPS
columns.description: The 5-digit FIPS code of the county in which the facility is located.

columns.name: Facility Type Description
columns.description: Type of facility (Hospital; Hospital Extension Clinic; Off-Campus ED; Primary Care Hospital-Critical Access Hospital).

columns.name: ED Encounters Requiring Ambulatory Surgery
columns.description: The number of ED encounters at the facility that required use of an operating room for ambulatory surgery.  Redacted values are represented with an ‘S’.

description: This dataset is a summary of the emergency department (ED) encounters reported in SPARCS data, summarized by facility and quarter of visit. ED encounters are classified according to the claim type associated with the SPARCS record into treat and release ED visits, ED visits that required ambulatory surgery, and ED visits that resulted in an inpatient admission.

description: The Institutional Provider Network Data displays information on health facilities and ancillary service providers (for example: hospitals, labs, home care agencies) participating in health plan networks from April through June 2020. Plan network data is collected from Medicaid, Commercial, and Exchange plans on a quarterly basis by NYSoH, including managed care plans, as well as PPO/EPO plans. For more information, please visit: https://pndslookup.health.ny.gov.

columns.name: County
columns.description: Cases are assigned to a county based on this order of preference: 1) the patient’s address, 2) the ordering healthcare provider’s address, or 3) the ordering facility’s address.

columns.name: FIPS

columns.name: Centroid
columns.description: The mapping coordinates (latitude, longitude) for the center of the specified county.

columns.name: Count
columns.description: The number of laboratory-confirmed influenza cases reported to NYSDOH for the corresponding season, week, disease, and county.

columns.name: Age Group
columns.description: Influenza-associated hospitalizations are aggregated by age group (0-4  years, 5-17 years, 18-49 years, 50-64 years, and 65 year and older)

columns.name: Week Ending Date
columns.description: The last date of each CDC week. Each week begins on Sunday and ends on Saturday; week ending dates are always on a Saturday.

columns.name: CDC Week
columns.description: CDC designates each week of the year with a sequential number starting with 1 to a maximum of 52 or 53. Week 1 is the first week of the year that has at least four days in the calendar year. CDC defines the influenza season as beginning with week 40 (generally the first week in October) and ending with week 20 of the following calendar year (generally the third week in May). Also known as MMWR week.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The five regions in New York are defined by county as Capital District Region counties: Albany, Clinton, Columbia, Delaware, Essex, Franklin, Fulton, Greene, Hamilton, Montgomery, Otsego, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie, Warren, Washington Central Region counties: Broome, Cayuga, Chenango, Cortland, Herkimer, Jefferson, Lewis, Madison, Oneida, Onondaga, Oswego, St Lawrence, Tioga, Tompkins Metropolitan Region counties: Dutchess, Nassau, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Suffolk, Sullivan, Ulster, Westchester New York City counties/boroughs: Bronx, Kings, New York, Queens, Richmond Western Region counties: Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Chemung, Erie, Genesee, Livingston, Monroe, Niagara, Ontario, Orleans, Schuyler, Seneca, Steuben, Wayne, Wyoming, Yates.

columns.name: Season
columns.description: Influenza season is named for the two calendar years over which a single influenza epidemic spans. CDC defines the influenza season as beginning with week 40 (generally the first week in October) of one calendar year and ending with week 20 of the following calendar year (generally the third week in May).

description: This dataset includes weekly counts of laboratory-confirmed influenza (flu) cases by age group.

name: Hospital Inpatient Discharges (SPARCS De-Identified): Maternal Sepsis by Select Risk Factors: 2016-2018
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Any Sepsis Crude Odds Ratio (95% CI)
columns.description: The unadjusted odds ratio from a logistic regression model predicting  any sepsis (including septicemia as well as severe sepsis/septic shock) during the specified maternal window for the specified risk factor strata  relative to the reference group, followed by the 95% confidence  interval (lower bound-upper bound) for the odds ratio. The reference  group for each risk factor is noted as “ref”.

columns.name: Risk Factor Type
columns.description: The general type of risk factor represented by the data within the row  and the coding definition used, where applicable (e.g., Bateman1 comorbidities, Elixhauser2 comorbidities).

columns.name: Maternal Window
columns.description: The period in which sepsis was identified: pregnancy, delivery, or  postpartum (within 42 days after delivery).

columns.name: Any Sepsis Incidence per 100,000 Live Births
columns.description: The rate of maternal sepsis events (including septicemia as well as severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding during the  specified maternal window and risk factor strata per 100,000 eligible  live births.

columns.name: Any Sepsis Incidence (N)
columns.description: The number of maternal sepsis events (including septicemia as well as  severe sepsis/septic shock) identified via diagnosis coding during the  specified maternal window among eligible live births within the  specified risk factorstrata.

columns.name: Live Births (N)
columns.description: The number of eligible live births events identified within the specified  risk factorstrata.

columns.name: Risk Factor Strata
columns.description: The strata of the risk factor characteristic represented by the data  within the row.

columns.name: Risk Factor
columns.description: The specific risk factor characteristic represented by the data within the  row.

columns.name: Data Source
columns.description: The data source used to identify the risk factor represented by the data  within the row: SPARCS (defined using ICD10 diagnoses) and/or birth  certificate

columns.name: Severe Sepsis Incidence per 100,000 Live Births
columns.description: The rate of maternal severe sepsis/septic shock events identified via  diagnosis coding at any point within the specified maternal window and  risk factor strata per 100,000 eligible live births.

columns.name: Year(s) of Live Birth
columns.description: The years during which the live births represented in the data occurred.

columns.name: Live Births (%)
columns.description: The percentage of eligible live births events within the specified risk  factor strata.

columns.name: Severe Sepsis p-value
columns.description: The p-value from the logistic regression model predicting severe  sepsis/septic shock during the specified maternal window for the  specified risk factor strata. Values below .05 indicate a statistically  significant association.

columns.name: Severe Sepsis Crude Odds Ratio (95% CI)
columns.description: The unadjusted odds ratio from a logistic regression model predicting  severe sepsis/septic shock during the specified maternal window for  the specified risk factor strata relative to the reference group, followed  by the 95% confidence interval (lower bound-upper bound) for the  odds ratio. The reference group for each risk factoris noted as “ref”.

columns.name: Severe Sepsis Incidence (N)
columns.description: The number of maternal severe sepsis/septic shock events identified  via diagnosis coding during the specified maternal window among eligible live births within the specified risk factor strata.

columns.name: Any Sepsis p-value
columns.description: The p-value from the logistic regression model predicting any sepsis (including septicemia as well assevere sepsis/septic shock) during the  specified maternal window for the specified risk factor strata. Values  below .05 indicate a statistically significant association.

description: The dataset contains counts, rates, and measures of association between select risk factors and administratively identified maternal sepsis among live births during the pregnancy, delivery, and postpartum windows between 2016 and 2018. Maternal sepsis is a leading cause of maternal mortality in the United States and is associated with increased rates of preterm labor, preterm delivery and fetal infection and maternal chronic pain and fertility problems. description: This dataset contains monthly snapshots of the number of enrollments by zip code in the New York State Donate Life Registry beginning in September 2008. The registry is a confidential database that records a person’s consent to organ/tissue donation upon death. The database is used by donation professionals to determine a person's donation status at the time of their death. For more information, please visit https://donatelife.ny.gov/.

provider: health.data.ny.gov
description: This dataset is used for mapping purposes, and does not contain any data.

name: Risk of Infected Blacklegged Tick Encounter: Adults (Oct to Dec): Beginning 2008
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: NY Region
columns.description: The region where ticks (all species and life stages) were collected. Ticks are collected from one or more publicly accessible sites per county sampled. Regions based on the “I Love NY” tourism regions.

columns.name: Total Sites Visited
columns.description: The total number of publicly accessible sites visited in the region. For the adult tick dataset, sites were visited from September-November.

columns.name: Tick Risk Score
columns.description: Relative risk of encountering an adult deer tick infected with any pathogen in the region. Risk score is the product of average adult deer tick density and the prevalence of infected adult deer ticks divided by 100.

columns.name: Total Tested
columns.description: Total number of adult deer ticks that were tested for the following: Borrelia burgdorferi (Lyme disease bacteria), Anaplasma phagocytophilum (anaplasmosis bacteria), Babesia microti (parasite causing babesiosis), Borrelia miyamotoi (relapsing fever bacteria). Ticks are tested individually.

columns.name: Average Adult Density
columns.description: The average number of adult deer ticks (also known as blacklegged ticks or their scientific name Ixodes scapularis) collected per 100 square meters of area sampled in the region. Tick population density is calculated only from the total adult deer ticks collected (and does not include other species or life stages collected at the time of the site visit).

columns.name: Prevalence of infected ticks
columns.description: The proportion of adult deer ticks that were positive for at least one pathogen. Prevalence is calculated by dividing the number of adult deer ticks infected with any pathogen by the total number of adult deer ticks collected and tested from the region during visits to publicly accessible sites from September-November and multiplying this number 100 to show a percentage.

description: Tick-borne diseases, such as Lyme disease, anaplasmosis and babesiosis, are a significant issue in New York (NY), impacting well over 10,000 individuals yearly. The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) has a responsibility to perform surveillance to monitor and track different kinds of tick-borne diseases. While most surveillance is done via reportable cases of human illness, tick-borne diseases can also be monitored by collecting ticks from the environment (called active surveillance) a

name: Risk of Infected Blacklegged Tick Encounter: Nymphs (May to Sep): Beginning 2008
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Site Total
columns.description: The total number of ticks (all species and life stages) collected in the region during visits to publicly accessible sites. For the nymph tick dataset, sites were visited from May-September.

columns.name: Prevalence of infected ticks
columns.description: The proportion of nymph deer ticks that were positive for at least one pathogen. Prevalence is calculated by dividing the total number of positive nymph deer ticks by the total number of nymph deer ticks collected and tested from the region during visits to publicly accessible sites from May-September and multiplying this number by 100 to show a percentage.

columns.name: Total Tested
columns.description: Total number of nymphal deer ticks that were tested for the following: Borrelia burgdorferi (Lyme disease bacteria), Anaplasma phagocytophilum (anaplasmosis bacteria), Babesia microti (parasite causing babesiosis), Borrelia miyamotoi (relapsing fever bacteria). Ticks are tested individually.

columns.name: NY Region
columns.description: The region where ticks (all species and life stages) were collected. Ticks are collected from one or more publicly accessible sites per county sampled. Regions based on the “I Love NY” tourism regions.

columns.name: Average Nymph Density
columns.description: The average number of nymph deer ticks (also known as blacklegged ticks or their scientific name Ixodes scapularis) collected per 100 square meters of area sampled in the region. Tick population density is calculated only from the total nymph deer ticks collected (and does not include other species or life stages collected at the time of the site visit).

columns.description: Number of prescriptions filled aggregated in this public use file.

columns.description: 4-digit Year in which prescriptions were  filled.

description: This dataset is designed to analyze prescription drug use and spending among New York State residents at the drug product level (pharmacy claims data that have been aggregated by labeler code and product code segments of the National Drug Code). The dataset includes the number of prescriptions filled by unique members by payer type, nonproprietary name, labeler name, dosage characteristics, amount insurer paid, and more.

Patient information collected as part of the HERDS Hospital Survey are lab-confirmed COVID-19 positive. name: Quality Assurance Reporting Requirements (QARR) Health Disparities 2022
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: The measures in QARR are divided into seven domains: Provider,  Network, Access to Primary Care, Child and Adolescent Health,  Women’s Health, Adult Health, Behavioral Health, and Satisfaction with  Care. Information from the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare  Providers and Systems (CAHPS) consumer satisfaction survey is  included in the Provider Network, Adults Living with Illness, as well as in  the Satisfaction with Care sections.

columns.name: Denominator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting denominator criteria specified by HEDIS within each characteristic

columns.name: Category
columns.description: This field reflects characteristics that are extracted from the member  information that is collected during enrollment and is linked to QARR  member-level data. The categories of demographic information  include: members’ sex, age, race, Medicaid aid category, payer status,  cash assistance status, mental health condition, substance use, and  region of residence.

columns.name: Characteristics
columns.description: This field reflects the range of values that are appropriate for each  demographic category described above

columns.name: Numerator
columns.description: Number of persons meeting numerator criteria within each  characteristic.

columns.description: Category within the domain with more detailed information regarding a  certain aspect of quality.

columns.name: Measure
columns.description: Measure short name that corresponds to the short name in the QARR  dataset file.

columns.name: Method of Data Collection
columns.description: Methods of collecting quality data are administrative review, hybrid  review, and the CAHPS survey. Administrative review utilizes  administrative systems only to identify the eligible population,  numerator events, and exclusions to calculate a rate. Hybrid review  utilizes administrative systems to use a sample of the eligible  population in addition to medical chart review. The CAHPS survey is an  annual survey that asks consumers and patients to report on and  evaluate their experiences with health care.

columns.name: Rate
columns.description: Percentage of numerator compliant within the denominator criteria.

columns.name: Measurement Year
columns.description: The time period the data represent.

columns.name: Measure Description
columns.description: Description of quality measure.

description: The Student Weight Status Category Reporting System (SWSCR) collects weight status category data (underweight, healthy weight, overweight or obese, based on BMI-for-age percentile) on children and adolescents attending public school in New York State, outside New York City. The dataset includes separate estimates of the percent of students that are overweight, obese and overweight or obese for all reportable grades within the county and/or region and by grade groups (elementary and middle/high).

name: New York State COVID-19 and Influenza Vaccination Data
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.name: Week Ending
columns.description: Doses are reported at a weekly aggregation, with dates based on vaccine administration date. ‘Week Ending’ date is the last day of each week’s reporting period.

columns.name: Respiratory Season
columns.description: COVID-19 and Influenza season begins in the summer of each year and continues through the spring of the new year. This data updates to a new ‘season’ when the next year’s vaccine is released. COVID-19 and Influenza vaccines are released on different schedules- this will result in COVID-19 and Influenza having slightly different season lengths.

columns.name: Geography Description
columns.description: The county or region of residence of the vaccinated individual.

columns.name: COVID-19 Dose Count
columns.description: Represents the total number of individuals who have received a dose of COVID-19 vaccine during a given season/reporting period.

columns.name: Influenza Dose Count
columns.description: Represents the total number of individuals who have received a dose of Influenza vaccine during a given season/report period.

columns.name: Geography Level
columns.description: Identifies the level of geographical aggregation of a record – county, region, or statewide (excluding NYC).

description: This dataset reports the number of people vaccinated by New York providers who have received a dose of the annual COVID-19 vaccine and the number who have received the annual Influenza vaccine, beginning with the 2024-2025 season. Note: This dataset replaces two prior COVID-19 vaccination datasets. Please refer to the notes section below for links to the archived data.

description: This dataset includes weekly aggregate counts of persons hospitalized with laboratory-confirmed influenza (flu), by county beginning with the 2020-21 season. The primary goal of this dataset is to provide users timely information about influenza-associated hospitalizations throughout the current influenza season. Although each influenza season is unique, historical data is provided for trend comparison.

description: The dataset includes information on operational, occupied, and available staffed bed counts at the reporting facility level. name: Emergency Department Treat and Release Encounters by Facility and Payer (SPARCS De-identified): Beginning 2018
provider: New York State Department of Health

columns.description: The percentage of visits at the facility by payer.

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: Type of Payer (0 Dual, 1 Medicare, 2 Medicaid, 3 Commercial, 4  Managed Care Unspecified, 5 Other, 6 Self-Pay, 9 Unknown).

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Indicates the year of the Emergency Department (ED) treat and  release encounter; derived from the date the patient was admitted  at the facility.

columns.name: Permanent Facility ID
columns.description: Site-specific Permanent Facility Identifier (PFI).

columns.name: Facility Name
columns.description: The name of the facility where services were provided based on the  Facility Identifier, as maintained by the NYSDOH Division of Health  Facility Planning.

columns.name: Facility Type
columns.description: Type of facility (Hospital; Hospital Extension Clinic; Off-Campus ED;  Primary Care Hospital-Critical Access Hospital).

columns.name: Facility County FIPS
columns.description: The 5-digit FIPS code of the county in which the facility is located.

columns.name: Facility County
columns.description: The name of the county in which the facility is located.

columns.name: Facility Region
columns.description: Region in which the facility is located (New York City, Outside of  New York City).

columns.name: Operating Certificate Number
columns.description: Operating Certificate number for the facility.

columns.name: Operator Name
columns.description: Name of the operator of the facility.

columns.name: Average Charges
columns.description: The average or mean charges amount at the facility by payer, ($).

columns.name: 75th Percentile Charges
columns.description: The 75th percentile charges amount at the facility by payer, ($).

columns.name: 25th Percentile Charges
columns.description: The 25th percentile charges amount at the facility by payer, ($).

columns.name: Median Charges Relative to Statewide
columns.description: The median charges at the facility by payer relative to statewide  (Above or Below).

columns.name: Difference bw Facility and Statewide Median Charges
columns.description: The difference of median charges by payer between the facility and statewide payer distribution (Facility Median Charges – Statewide  Median Charges), ($).

columns.name: Median Charges
columns.description: The median charges amount at the facility by payer, ($).

columns.name: Charges
columns.description: The total charges amount at the facility by payer, ($).

columns.name: Percent of Visits Relative to Statewide
columns.description: The percentage of visits at the facility by payer relative to statewide payer distribution (Above or Below).

columns.name: Difference bw Facility and Statewide Percent of Visits
columns.description: The difference of percentage of visits by payer between the facility and statewide (Facility Percent – Statewide Percent).

description: Emergency department (ED) encounters are classified according to the claim type associated with the SPARCS record into treat and release ED visits, ED visits that required ambulatory surgery, and ED visits that resulted in an inpatient admission. This dataset is a summary of the ED treat and release encounters reported in SPARCS data, summarized by facility and payer.

name: Opioid Excise Tax Annual Report: 2022
provider: New York State Department of Health
description: Article 20-D of New York State Tax Law establishes an excise tax on the sale of opioids. In compliance with the Opioid Tax, each registrant (e.g., manufacturer, wholesaler, or outsourcing facility) shall provide a report to the Department of Health detailing all opioid transactions into or within New York State. For addtional information visit: https://www.tax.ny.gov/bus/oet/oetidx.htm
